channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] World Skydiving Day takes flight at Skydive City in Zephyrhills
item.description: Tens of thousands of people took to the skies Saturday as World Skydiving Day united first-time jumpers and experienced skydivers at drop zones around the world.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Why has the US restarted strikes against Iran now?
item.description: The United States launched a third straight day of strikes against Iran Monday, telling lawmakers the measures were meant to defend U.S. interests and civilian traffic against aggression.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] News Tonight - Top Headlines and Weather for July 13, 2026
item.description: Family seeks answers after teen found dead in fitting room and Dick's House of Sports, Florida’s attorney general launched an investigation into flour additive, storms continue this week.

item.title: [WATCHLIST: hurricane] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] $60M in infrastructure upgrades to St. Pete facility to protect sewage system from future hurricanes
item.description: St. Petersburg officials have finished more than $60 million in infrastructure upgrades at the Northeast Water Reclamation Facility to safeguard the city's sewage system from future hurricane storm surges.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Near-record temperatures and heat index values could approach advisory levels
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Florida faculty hail Stop WOKE Act court ruling

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa Bay locals speak out about Cuba's worsening blackouts, food shortages, and water crisis
item.description: A Zephyrhills woman and other Tampa Bay locals share their reaction to Cuba's worsening blackouts, food shortages, and water crisis.

item.title: [WATCHLIST: city council] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Clearwater Duke Energy agreement: City council considers 30-year deal
item.description: Clearwater officials are evaluating a long-term utility partnership after months of debate over whether to establish a municipal power network or maintain ties with Duke Energy.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Teen takeover trend reaches Manatee County after large gathering at Bradenton park
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Manatee County credit card skimming investigation leads to arrest, 12 skimming devices found at suspect’s home
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Golf cart battery fire at Bent Tree Golf Club prompts lithium-ion safety warning

[WATCHLIST: city council] [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Duke Energy proposes 30-year agreement with City of Clearwater following utility debate
item.description: Clearwater City Council is considering a new 30-year agreement with Duke Energy. The discussions came after the City considered operating its own public utility service.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Facing a slow summer, Dunedin businesses push for downtown 'sip and stroll'
item.description: More than a dozen downtown Dunedin restaurants, bars and breweries are asking city leaders to consider changes they believe could help attract more visitors.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 'Those Days Are Over': Manatee County residents voice traffic concerns as growth continues
item.description: Bradenton community members shared frustrations about congestion and infrastructure at listening event; county outlines planning approach

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] AdventHealth and Pepin Foundation donate AED to Tampa homeless center where one man found hope and purpose
item.description: April McCullus, Director of Community Resiliency with Well Built Cities, said people experiencing homelessness face elevated rates of chronic illness and significant barriers to healthcare.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida's Hometown Heroes Program reopens, offering up to $35,000 for eligible first-time homebuyers
item.description: The state’s Hometown Heroes Housing Program reopened Monday with $50 million in funding available to help eligible first-time homebuyers with down payment and closing cost assistance.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] US strikes targets in Iran for third straight day as blockade resumes
item.description: The U.S. continued strikes against Iran Monday, the same day it reinstated a blockade against Iranian shipping and ports.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] First lawsuit filed against Publix over recalled GreenWise blueberries tied to E. coli outbreak
item.description: A national food poisoning law firm has filed the first lawsuit against Publix over recalled frozen blueberries linked to an E. coli outbreak, according to attorneys representing the plaintiffs.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida Democrats say 2026 momentum can break GOP supermajorities
item.description: Florida Democrats say they have proof a 2026 comeback is possible. Now, they have to prove that momentum can survive Election Day.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] South Carolina governor appoints Lindsey Graham's sister to serve out his term
item.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster announced Monday that he would appoint Darline Graham Nordone, Lindsey Graham's sister, to serve out the remainder of his term in the Senate.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida man with rare blood cancer fights insurer over insurance policy keeping him from medication
item.description: Man says his insurance company refused to count nearly $9,000 in drug manufacturer assistance toward his deductible through a “co-accumulator” policy.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Father looking for answers regarding teenage son's death inside Dick's House of Sports in Brandon
item.description: On Friday, 15-year-old Jaden Law was found dead inside a fitting room at the Dick's House of Sports at Brandon Exchange following gunshots.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Pasco family's gift creates recovery program at Saint Leo inspired by son's legacy
item.description: Dewey and Becky Mitchell hope the Derek J. Mitchell Collegiate Recovery Program will help students overcome addiction as they pursue a college education.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Investigation launched into flour additive possibly linked to cancer: Florida AG
item.description: Florida’s attorney general launched an investigation into food products containing potassium bromate, seeking records tied to products sold to consumers and schools across the state.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] The last thing a Russian tanker sees: Inside Ukraine's drone war at sea
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck roughly 90 Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov over seven days, part of an intensifying campaign to sever the fuel lifeline to Russian-occupied Crimea.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] The World Cup’s final four is set. Here's who's in as the final week begins
item.description: The World Cup semifinals are set. 🌍⚽ England, Argentina, France, and Spain are the last four standing. Who do you think lifts the trophy on July 19?

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 24-year-old road ranger hit, killed while working crash in Pasco County: FHP
item.description: The Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) said a road ranger was hit and killed while working the scene of a crash on Sunday night in Pasco County.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Lakeland private school administrator accused of striking a deputy at traffic stop: PCSO
item.description: Polk County Sheriff’s Office (PCSO) said a Lakeland middle school assistant director is accused of striking a deputy on the hand and resisting arrest at a traffic stop.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Man who killed Dartmouth professors at 17 has chance at parole in about 20 years
item.description: A Vermont man who was 17 when he and a friend killed a pair of married Dartmouth College professors 25 years ago will have a chance at parole in about 20 years, when he reaches the age of one of his victims, a judge ruled Monday.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] States sue to block Paramount’s Warner Bros. Discovery takeover
item.description: Twelve states have sued to block Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery, kicking off a high-stakes antitrust battle that could impede the media mega-merger.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Wildfires across Europe have killed hundreds over the last decade
item.description: Wildfires raging across Europe have killed hundreds of people over the last decade, and climate change is set to push the death toll even higher in the coming years.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Things to do in Tampa Bay this weekend | July 17-19
item.description: If you're looking for something fun to do this weekend (July 17-19), there's plenty of action happening around the Tampa Bay area.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Judge says Trump’s IRS lawsuit was filed for ‘improper purpose'
item.description: A federal judge has found that President Donald Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service over the leak of his tax returns was filed for an “improper purpose.”

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Man tackled by deputy in Brooksville Walmart faces aggravated assault with firearm charge: HCSO
item.description: A man is facing an aggravated assault with a firearm charge after a multi-county incident ends with him being tackled inside a Walmart with a bible.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] From near-tragedy to graduation day, this teen has a remarkable journey
item.description: In 2010, a 2‑year‑old boy was critically injured after a large rock fell and struck him in the head during a family visit to a Tennessee state park. Now, his family is celebrating a major milestone.

item.title: [BREAKING] [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Man arrested, charged with murder in 2025 fatal club shooting: St. Pete police
item.description: St. Petersburg Police Department announced Monday the arrest of the suspect tied to the fatal shooting of a 27-year-old man back in 2025.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Suspect shot by deputies after domestic violence incident in Auburndale: PCSO
item.description: Polk County Sheriff's Office said a deputy-involved shooting occurred in Auburndale after a domestic violence incident.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Ukraine, 9 others announce coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles
item.description: Ukraine as well as 9 other countries have announced a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Is there such a thing as being too old to execute? Aging death row inmates are set to die in Florida
item.description: The last prisoner strapped to a table in Florida’s death chamber was 74 years old — the oldest the state has executed in modern times.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump reinstates Iranian blockade, says Strait of Hormuz will stay open
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that the Strait of Hormuz is open and will remain open, as he ordered the U.S. military to reinstate a blockade on Iranian ships and ports.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Catching Rays with Craig Kimbrel
item.description: Tampa Bay 28 sports anchor Kyle Burger grabbed a glove and played catch with Kimbrel under the Tropicana Field roof.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Beat the Streets Tampa Bay: Non-profit partners with USF wrestling coach for camp
item.description: A Tampa Bay non-profit is partnering with the University of South Florida men's wrestling coach to bring children from underserved communities to a wrestling camp at Fivay High School.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Longtime Tampa youth robotics team hosts first-ever summer camp fundraiser
item.description: Team Duct Tape, a community-based youth robotics team, hosted its first-ever summer camp to spark a passion for engineering.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Operation Southern Slowdown targets Florida 'super speeder' drivers
item.description: State and local law enforcement agencies are officially easing off the gas and ramping up enforcement as they kick off "Operation Southern Slowdown."

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Ex-Temple Terrace police officer enters written plea of not guilty in crash that killed 6-year-old girl
item.description: Former Temple Terrace police officer Zachary Krug entered a written plea of not guilty on charges stemming from a deadly crash that killed a 6-year-old girl last April.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Pete police arrest man in 2025 homicide
item.description: St. Petersburg police officers arrested Jermaine Ivery on Monday for first-degree murder following a fatal shooting outside a local nightclub in Feb. 2025.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] 2 hospitalized after shooting at Clearwater sports bar: Police
item.description: Clearwater police are investigating a shooting that left two people hospitalized early Monday morning at a downtown sports bar.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Tampa Bay Rays retire Evan Longoria's No. 3 jersey
item.description: After being traded from the Rays in 2018, Evan Longoria never got to return to Tropicana Field in any official capacity. That is, until now.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Mote Marine Laboratory finds disease-resistant coral that could help Florida reef restoration
item.description: Scientists at Mote Marine Laboratory have discovered disease-resistant coral strains that could prove vital to restoring Florida's severely depleted reef ecosystems. The breakthrough follows a four-year study targeting one of the fastest-spreading and most destructive marine outbreaks in recent history.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Bobby Jones Nature Park: City unveils first phase upgrades
item.description: The Bobby Jones Nature Park in Sarasota is reaching a new milestone in conservation as city officials open the first stage of the environmental restoration project. This transformation of the historic local golf club property marks the culmination of nearly a decade of planning and community effort to protect the area for future generations.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Venice man accused of shooting man outside sports bar: SCSO
item.description: A 24-year-old man was arrested after he shot a man in the leg during an argument outside a Venice sports bar Sunday night, according to the Sarasota County Sheriff's Office.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Polk County animal rescue battles daily wave of abandoned cats
item.description: A local animal rescue group is sounding the alarm over a massive surge in abandoned cats across Polk County while calling for increased funding and new community solutions.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Deputy shoots at driver who rammed cruiser head-on in Auburndale: Grady Judd
item.description: A head-on crash on Fowler Avenue sparked a deputy-involved shooting in the Sun Acres neighborhood.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Bradenton man accused of hitting, killing FDOT Road Ranger on I-75: FHP
item.description: A Florida Department of Transportation Road Ranger was hit and killed while assisting at a crash scene on Interstate 75, and the driver accused of hitting him has been arrested on a DUI manslaughter charge, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Man carrying Bible tackled in Hernando County Walmart after leaving 2 guns on car: Sheriff
item.description: A suspect is in custody following a multi-county pursuit Monday morning that ended when an undercover deputy tackled the man inside a Hernando County Walmart customer service area.

item.title: [Tampa Bay Beacon (Pinellas)] Things to do around Pinellas: July 16-22
[Tampa Bay Beacon (Pinellas)] Tarpon Springs issues call to artists
